question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open an unbounded number of TCP connections to port 5600 (no global connection cap is set on the NettyServerBuilder) and hold each connection open with idle streams, exhausting the server's file descriptor and socket limits, causing the gRPC server to refuse all new connections and preventing any transaction confirmation from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionAge or maxConnectionAgeGrace settings on the NettyServerBuilder to hold long-lived connections open indefinitely, accumulating state and memory until the JVM heap is exhausted and the gRPC service crashes, causing a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user saturate all 5 concurrent call slots per connection (maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection = 5) across thousands of simultaneous connections, consuming the entire applicationTaskExecutor thread pool so that no legitimate transaction-confirmation gRPC calls can be scheduled, resulting in a total service outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open many connections and on each connection immediately open exactly 5 concurrent streaming calls (the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection limit), keeping each stream alive with periodic flow-control window updates but never consuming data, thereby pinning all executor threads and starving legitimate callers from confirming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the shared applicationTaskExecutor is used for all gRPC I/O dispatch (serverBuilder.executor(applicationTaskExecutor)) to submit slow or blocking gRPC calls that monopolize the shared thread pool, starving Spring's own async processing and preventing transaction-confirmation events from being dispatched?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a slow-read attack by opening a gRPC streaming subscription and then deliberately not reading response frames, causing the server-side write buffer to fill and the executor thread handling that call to block indefinitely, eventually exhausting all applicationTaskExecutor threads and halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of keepAliveTime or keepAliveTimeout configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to keep zombie connections alive after the underlying TCP session has silently dropped, accumulating phantom connections that hold executor threads and prevent new transaction-confirmation calls from being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxInboundMessageSize on the NettyServerBuilder to send an extremely large gRPC request frame, causing the server to allocate a massive byte buffer per call, triggering an OutOfMemoryError in the JVM and crashing the gRPC service entirely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxInboundMetadataSize on the NettyServerBuilder to send gRPC requests with extremely large header frames (e.g., thousands of custom metadata key-value pairs), causing per-call heap allocation to spike and eventually exhaust JVM memory, shutting down the server?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a flowControlWindow setting on the NettyServerBuilder to send data faster than the server can process it, overwhelming the Netty event loop with buffered frames and causing the server to become unresponsive to all new transaction-confirmation requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of permitKeepAliveTime or permitKeepAliveWithoutCalls settings to send a flood of HTTP/2 PING frames, forcing the server to respond to each one and consuming Netty event-loop CPU cycles, degrading throughput to the point where transaction confirmations time out?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of HTTP/2 RST_STREAM frames (rapid stream reset attack) against the gRPC server, exploiting the absence of any RST_STREAM rate-limiting configuration on the NettyServerBuilder, causing the Netty event loop to spend all CPU time processing resets and starving legitimate transaction-confirmation streams?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConcurrentStreams at the HTTP/2 transport level (distinct from maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection at the gRPC layer) to open more streams than the gRPC layer enforces, causing inconsistency between transport and application-layer limits and allowing more threads to be consumed than intended?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor (not a dedicated gRPC executor) to create a cross-component interference attack, where flooding the gRPC endpoint with calls also starves Spring's scheduled tasks (e.g., importer polling), indirectly halting transaction ingestion and confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a large number of concurrent gRPC subscription calls that each acquire a read-only database transaction via transactionOperationsReadOnly(), exhausting the database connection pool so that no write transactions (needed for transaction confirmation ingestion) can be obtained, causing a total halt in transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user open many long-lived gRPC streaming subscriptions that each hold a read-only TransactionTemplate-managed database connection open for the duration of the stream, depleting the connection pool and blocking the importer from writing new transaction records, effectively halting network confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate (setReadOnly(true)) to trigger repeated slow database queries within a single transaction context, holding a DB connection open for an extended period and starving the connection pool of available connections needed for transaction confirmation writes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a handshakeTimeout on the NettyServerBuilder to initiate thousands of TCP connections that complete the TCP handshake but never complete the HTTP/2 or TLS handshake, holding server-side connection state indefinitely and exhausting available connection slots?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any IP-based connection rate limiting in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to perform a connection-flood attack from a single IP, opening thousands of connections per second and overwhelming the server's accept queue before any application-level logic can reject them?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection is applied per-connection (not globally) to bypass the intended concurrency limit by simply opening N connections and running 5 calls on each, achieving N*5 concurrent server threads with no global cap, exhausting the applicationTaskExecutor?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionIdle configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to keep idle connections alive indefinitely, accumulating thousands of idle connections that each consume kernel socket buffers and JVM memory, eventually causing an OOM or resource exhaustion that shuts down the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed HTTP/2 SETTINGS frames to the gRPC server, exploiting the absence of any custom HTTP/2 frame validation in the NettyServerBuilder configuration, causing Netty to enter an error-handling loop that consumes event-loop threads and degrades service availability for transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a global call deadline enforcement in the grpcServerConfigurer bean to initiate streaming calls that never terminate, holding executor threads indefinitely and preventing new transaction-confirmation calls from being scheduled?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxCallStartDelay or similar timeout on the NettyServerBuilder to send a gRPC request that opens a stream but never sends the initial request message, holding the stream open and consuming an executor thread slot until the server is fully saturated?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the applicationTaskExecutor is a bounded thread pool (Spring default) by submitting exactly enough concurrent gRPC calls to fill the queue, causing subsequent legitimate calls to be rejected with RejectedExecutionException, which propagates as gRPC UNAVAILABLE errors and halts transaction confirmation?
